desc: Budva is Montenegro's Riviera base: a compact walled old town linked to busy summer beaches, boat excursions, and one of the Adriatic's livelier nightlife scenes. It peaks hard in July–August, while late spring and early autumn offer warmer swimming conditions with less crowd pressure and easier transfer logistics.
hls: Walled Old Town on the sea
Beaches and coves within walking distance
Boat trips to Sveti Nikola Island
Great base for the Budva Riviera

todo.name: Stari Grad (Old Town)
todo.desc: Explore limestone alleys, tiny squares, and Venetian-era walls in one of the Adriatic's most photogenic historic cores.

todo.name: Mogren Beach coastal path
todo.desc: Short cliffside walk from town to two clear-water coves linked by a tunnel through the rocks.

todo.name: Citadel & town ramparts
todo.desc: Climb for panoramas over Budva Bay, red-tiled roofs, and the string of beaches southward.

todo.name: Sveti Nikola Island boat
todo.desc: Five to ten minutes by taxi-boat to pebbly coves and pine shade opposite Budva's waterfront.

todo.name: Sveti Stefan viewpoint
todo.desc: Visit the classic terrace views above Montenegro's iconic islet-hotel and pink-pebble shoreline.

todo.name: Slovenska Plaza promenade
todo.desc: Long seafront strip for sunset walks, casual dining, and easy access to multiple town beaches.

todo.name: Jaz Beach
todo.desc: Wider bay west of town known for concerts, open-water swimming, and better space in peak summer.

todo.name: Reževići & Praskvica trails
todo.desc: Olive-grove and monastery paths in the hills above the coast for a quieter, inland contrast.

wifi.note: Generally good in apartments/hotels and seafront cafés; evening slowdowns are common in peak summer when network demand surges.

prac.fltNote: No regular nonstop Gatwick route to Budva. Typical routings use Tivat (TIV) or Podgorica (TGD) via one stop; return fares are often ~£90–£190 off-peak, ~£190–£320 in shoulder demand, and ~£320+ during July–August peaks.
prac.visa: Visa-free for UK citizens for short stays (typically up to 90 days in a 180-day period).
alerts: Coastal road congestion can be severe on summer weekends; pad airport transfer time.
Beach service pricing varies widely by zone and season — check sunbed minimum spends before settling.
Old-town stays can be noisy in peak nightlife periods.

sweet: Late May to June and September are usually the best balance of swimmable sea, active resort services, and manageable crowds.
